summary: The bats test suite provides good unit-level coverage of core functionality (glob patterns, multi-label matching, priority/exclusive rules, error cases, help/comments), but violates the explicit architectural requirement that 'every single test case must execute through the GitHub Actions workflow via `act` — do NOT test your script directly.' Tests run directly via bats, not through the workflow. Coverage misses edge cases (empty files, whitespace variations, special characters, case sensitivity, CRLF handling in files/labels, very long inputs). Rigor is limited: happy paths and basic error cases tested, but boundary conditions underrepresented; no tests for unicode/symbol characters, paths with spaces, or glob-pattern edge cases. Design is well-structured locally (clear names, proper setup(), independent tests, good readability) but architecturally wrong (should orchestrate through act). The suite also doesn't verify shell quality requirements (shellcheck, bash -n validation) or workflow structure/actionlint compliance as required by the task. While individual tests are competent, the suite would fail to catch regressions that only manifest in the CI environment or with edge-case inputs.
